binaries.title: msisadrv.sys - KMDF static-stub rebuild and compiler stack-init churn

changed_functions.name: MsIsaEvtDeviceProcessQueryInterface (0x1C00062A0 / 0x1C00062B0)
key_changes: Decompiled body is byte-for-byte identical between builds; only the entry address (0x1C00062A0 -> 0x1C00062B0) and internal stack-slot allocation differ
The compiler assigns the first WDF-retrieval output to [rsp+0x44] (var_14) in the patched build vs [rsp+0x40] (var_18) in the unpatched build; the other 4-byte output slot is used correspondingly

Both builds pre-initialize to 0 the stack slot that is later read into ecx as the first argument to HalDispatchTable+0x78: unpatched 'mov [rsp+0x40], eax' (eax=0) at 0x1C00062E9, patched 'mov [rsp+0x44], esi' (esi=0) at 0x1C0006326
Patched build additionally saves/restores rsi and uses esi as a zero source; the WDF table offset (0x288), type indices (0xd, 0xe), and HalDispatchTable+0x78 target are unchanged
changed_functions.security_notes: This is the EvtDeviceProcessQueryInterface callback (registered in MsIsaEvtDeviceAdd via the WDF function at table offset 1824 against GUID_TRANSLATOR_INTERFACE_STANDARD), invoked on a kernel PnP IRP_MN_QUERY_INTERFACE, not an EvtIoDeviceControl handler and not reachable from a user-mode DeviceIoControl call. In both builds the value passed as the first argument to HalDispatchTable+0x78 is the 5th-parameter output of the first WDF-retrieval call, and that slot is written to 0 before the call in both builds. The remaining output slot is written by WDF but its value is never read in either build. There is no swapped-semantics bug, no uninitialized value reaches the HAL call, and nothing is leaked. The difference is compiler stack-slot reassignment plus zero-init of an address-taken local. No security-relevant change.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED @ 0x1C00062A0:
0x1c00062e9: mov [rsp+0x40], eax        ; var_18 = 0 (GUID match result)
0x1c00062ed: lea rcx, [rsp+0x44]        ; &var_14 (address-taken only, value never read)
0x1c00062ff: mov [rsp+0x28], rcx        ; 6th param slot = &var_14
0x1c0006307: lea rcx, [rsp+0x40]
0x1c000630c: mov [rsp+0x20], rcx        ; 5th param slot = &var_18
0x1c0006311: mov rax, [rax+0x288]
0x1c0006318: lea r8d, [r9+9]            ; type index 0xd
0x1c0006323: call [__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr]
0x1c00063ae: mov ecx, [rsp+0x40]        ; HAL arg1 = var_18 (pre-set to 0)
0x1c00063b2: call [__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr]  ; HalDispatchTable+0x78

PATCHED @ 0x1C00062B0:
0x1c00062bf: xor esi, esi
0x1c00062c4: mov [rsp+0x40], esi        ; var_18 = 0
0x1c0006311: lea rcx, [rsp+0x40]
0x1c0006316: mov [rsp+0x28], rcx        ; 6th param slot = &var_18
0x1c0006321: lea rcx, [rsp+0x44]
0x1c0006326: mov [rsp+0x44], esi        ; var_14 = 0 (zero-init)
0x1c000632a: mov [rsp+0x20], rcx        ; 5th param slot = &var_14
0x1c0006339: mov rax, [rax+0x288]
0x1c0006340: lea r8d, [r9+9]            ; type index 0xd
0x1c0006344: call [__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr]
0x1c00063cb: mov ecx, [rsp+0x44]        ; HAL arg1 = var_14 (pre-set to 0)
0x1c00063cf: call [__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr]  ; HalDispatchTable+0x78

changed_functions.name: MsIsaPnPIrpPreProcessingCallback (0x1C00063D0 / 0x1C00063F0)
key_changes: PnP IRP_MN_QUERY_BUS_INFORMATION (minor function 0x18) handler: pool-buffer initialization instructions reordered
Unpatched writes [rax+0x10]=0 then [rax+0x10]=1 then [rax+0x14]=busnum; patched writes [rax+0x10]=1 as a qword (also zeroing [rax+0x14]) then [rax+0x14]=busnum. Net buffer contents are identical
IoStatus.Status constant (0 on success) is materialized slightly earlier in the patched build; data section reference for the GUID constant relocated
changed_functions.security_notes: The callback allocates a 0x18-byte pool buffer (tag MIsa) holding GUID_BUS_TYPE_ISAPNP, a reference count, and the assigned bus number. In BOTH builds all 0x18 bytes are fully written before the buffer is published via IoStatus.Information, so there is no window of uninitialized pool residue in either build. The change is instruction ordering only, a code-generation refactor, not a security fix.

changed_functions.name: FxStubBindClasses (0x1C00011F4 / 0x1C0001224)
key_changes: KMDF static-stub rebuild: fixed 0x50 stride replaced with dynamic size iteration via new FxGetNextClassBindInfo helper
Added DbgPrintEx error logging on bind failures
changed_functions.security_notes: KMDF static-stub rebuild. Runs only during driver load, not on any attacker-controlled input path. Not security-relevant.

changed_functions.name: FxStubInitTypes (0x1C0001334 / 0x1C000147C)
key_changes: KMDF static-stub rebuild: fixed 0x28 stride replaced with dynamic size iteration
Added DbgPrintEx error logging
changed_functions.security_notes: KMDF static-stub rebuild. Runs only during driver initialization, not on attacker-controlled input paths. Not security-relevant.

changed_functions.name: FxStubUnbindClasses (0x1C00012B0 / 0x1C0001384)
key_changes: KMDF static-stub rebuild cleanup path using dynamic size iteration and the new FxGetNextClassBindInfo helper
Added DbgPrintEx on failure
changed_functions.security_notes: KMDF static-stub rebuild unload path. Not attacker-reachable.

changed_functions.name: FxGetNextClassBindInfo (new @ 0x1C000143C in patched)
key_changes: New KMDF static-stub helper that walks the class-bind table by dynamic size, used by the rebuilt FxStubBindClasses/FxStubUnbindClasses
changed_functions.security_notes: New framework helper introduced by the KMDF static-stub rebuild. Init/unload only. Not attacker-reachable.

changed_functions.name: FxDriverEntryWorker (0x1C00010C4)
key_changes: Added DbgPrintEx('DriverEntry failed 0x%x for driver %wZ') diagnostic on DriverEntry failure
Updated WDF data references (unk_1C0003150 -> unk_1C00031D0, qword_1C0003120 -> qword_1C00031A0)
changed_functions.security_notes: Rebuild with added diagnostics. The added DbgPrintEx is for debugging only, not a security fix.

changed_functions.name: MsIsaEvtDeviceAdd (0x1C0006010)
key_changes: Additional compiler zero-initialization of address-taken stack locals before use
Updated WDF data/callback references and improved zeroing patterns
changed_functions.security_notes: Init-only device-add routine. Extra stack zero-init is compiler stack-hardening churn, not a behavioral security change, and is not reachable from an untrusted input path.

changed_functions.name: DriverEntry (0x1C0007040)
key_changes: Compiler stack-initialization churn: the WDF config structure is zeroed with wider stores instead of individual mov instructions
Updated WDF data references
changed_functions.security_notes: Init-only routine. Compiler code-generation change, no behavioral security change.

changed_functions.name: memset / XFG thunk (0x1C0001400 -> import/thunk; new _guard_xfg_dispatch_icall_nop @ 0x1C00015A0)
key_changes: Compiler/linker artifact: inlined memset and CFG/XFG dispatch thunk changes from the rebuild
changed_functions.security_notes: Purely a compilation/linkage artifact. Not a security fix.

summary: No security-relevant change. The msisadrv.sys patched build differs from the unpatched build by a KMDF static-stub rebuild (FxStubBindClasses/FxStubInitTypes/FxStubUnbindClasses switched from fixed-stride to dynamic size iteration via a new FxGetNextClassBindInfo helper, with added DbgPrintEx diagnostics and an added XFG dispatch thunk) plus compiler stack-initialization churn (zero-init of address-taken locals and stack-slot reassignment). The two device-facing callbacks are functionally identical across builds: MsIsaEvtDeviceProcessQueryInterface (a kernel PnP EvtDeviceProcessQueryInterface callback, not a user-mode IOCTL handler) decompiles identically, and in both builds the value passed to HalDispatchTable+0x78 is pre-initialized to 0 and the second WDF output slot is never read; MsIsaPnPIrpPreProcessingCallback fully initializes its 0x18-byte pool buffer before publishing it in both builds. No attacker-reachable vulnerability was introduced or fixed.
